_comment: Consolidated held-out benchmark results transcribed from final.ipynb. GSM8K = full test (1319), StrategyQA = full test (687), MMLU = stratified 570-item sample (seed 42, 10/subject). Greedy decoding (do_sample=False). Base model evaluated in its native base_fewshot format; all think/answer checkpoints in their native chat format (think_fewshot is the primary native format; think_zeroshot reported for the SFT launch point).

protocol.gsm8k: full test, 1319 items, 8-shot CoT (base) / native think-answer (trained)

final_model.name: concise-Q4_K_XL.gguf (UD-Q4_K_XL, 5.09 GB)
final_model.lineage: Qwen2.5-7B -> PEAR-SFT -> anti-guess PROF-GRPO -> conditional-conciseness GRPO -> QAT 4-bit
final_model.note: Conciseness pass cut mean correct-trace length by ~8-10% with pool accuracy held; quantization KL (0.008) is below the 0.01 accept threshold, so held-out accuracy is within ~1pp of the bf16 GRPO model.

improvement_targets_+5pp.mmlu: -3.0 (held / no gain; not a training target)

kpi_assessment.verdict: All three minimum thresholds cleared with large margins. The >=+5pp improvement bar is met decisively on GSM8K; StrategyQA improves but falls ~3 pp short of the +5pp stretch target (see reward-hacking analysis in docs/methods-and-observations.md).
